a: A Quick Look preview request that indicates the content to preview.
A protocol that defines a set of properties you implement to make a preview of your application’s content.
A type that defines a block used to load a Quick Look preview item.
A class that implements the Quick Look preview panel to display a preview of a list of items.
A protocol that the Quick Look preview panel uses to access the contents of its data source object.
A protocol for the delegate of the Quick Look preview panel.
A class that you subclass to provide a data-based Quick Look preview extension.
The class you create when providing a data-based Quick Look preview extension.
An attachment for a Quick Look preview reply that provides additional content for the system to display a preview.
A Quick Look preview of an item that you can embed into your view hierarchy.
Styles for a Preview View.
Provides a compact preview style suited for smaller previews or inspectors.
Provides the default preview style for larger, full-sized previews.
A protocol for implementing a custom controller to create previews of files.
Create previews of files to use inside your macOS app.
The attachments for a preview reply that provide additional data for the system to display the preview.
A Boolean value that determines whether the preview starts automatically.
Closes the view, releasing the current preview item.
The content type of the preview attachment.
The current first responder accepting to control the preview panel.
The currently previewed item.
The index of the current preview item.
The data of the preview attachment.
The preview panel data source.
The delegate object that controls the preview panel’s behavior.
The preview panel’s display state.
The current display state of the [previewItem](/documentation/quicklookui/qlpreviewview/previewitem).
Instructs the panel to enter full screen mode.
Instructs the panel to exit full screen mode.
The URL that indicates the content to preview.
Creates a preview reply attachment with the specified type.
Creates a preview reply from an existing file URL.
Creates a preview view with the provided frame.
Creates a preview view with the provided frame and style.
The property that indicates whether the panel is in full screen mode.
Returns the number of items that the preview panel should preview.
Prepares the preview of a file at the specified file’s URL.
Prepares the preview for a file by using the data from Spotlight’s searchable item.
The item to preview.
The display state for the preview item.
The title to display for the preview item.
The URL of the item to preview.
Handles an event that the preview panel receives, but doesn’t handle.
Returns the item that the preview panel should preview at a given index.
Returns the screen rectangle for a given preview item.
Returns the image to use for the transition zoom effect for a given item.
Prepares the preview of a file identified within a file preview request.
Asks the preview panel to recompute the preview of the current preview item.
Updates the preview to display the currently previewed item.
Asks the preview panel to reload its data from its data source.
Returns the shared Quick Look preview panel instance.
Returns a Boolean value that indicates whether the system has created a shared Quick Look preview panel.
A Boolean value that determines whether the preview should close when its window closes.
String encoding for text or html based previews. Defaults to NSUTF8StringEncoding.
The title for the system to display with the preview.
Asks the preview panel to update its current controller.
